mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.9% in Feb 2011 suggests post-crisis recovery phase
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand and economic activity, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth rate exceeds 3-month moving average by 2% or more
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 387.1B in Feb 2011 reflects post-recession consumer recovery
failure_condition: Triggered signal produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near zero combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates negative real rates that compress bond yields and redirect capital flows into equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% and CPIAUCSL at 221.1 in early 2011
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side strength, which supports profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.1 in Jan 2011 reflects post-crisis manufacturing recovery
failure_condition: Triggered signal produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 54% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust labor market momentum that increases household income and consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130.5M and UNRATE at 8.9% in Feb 2011
regime_hint: labor market recovery with synchronized employment and unemployment improvement
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and forward returns becomes insignificant (p > 0.10) when UNRATE is not declining.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 600K units signals demand weakness and reduced construction activity, which constrains economic growth and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 600K units and 3-month trend is negative
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 596K in Jan 2011 reflects persistent housing weakness post-crisis
regime_hint: demand-constrained environment with housing sector stress
failure_condition: Triggered signal produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicates broad-based economic expansion that supports equity valuations and forward returns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2% annualized AND RSAFS 3-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13370.1B and RSAFS at 387.1B in Q4 2010/Feb 2011
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across GDP and consumption
failure_condition: Interaction signal produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 63% of triggered instances, or the joint predictive power is not significantly greater than either series alone (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity risk premiums and supports valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% in Feb 2011 reflects extended zero lower bound policy
regime_hint: monetary accommodation at zero lower bound
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during zero-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
